title: cornus macrophylla var. variegata
intro: Cornus macrophylla var. variegata, also known as the variegated flowering dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its attractive white and green variegated leaves and showy white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Cornus macrophylla var. variegata are attributed to its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce cholesterol, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable herbal remedy for managing various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Cornus macrophylla var. variegata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from fever and inflammation to digestive issues and skin conditions.
